summary: Initialized Orchestrator project and session files, refactored the Rust CLI into safer testable units, added regression coverage for matching and playlist input parsing, and rewrote the README to match the real product. No commits were made during the session; the repo is improved substantially but still needs a live Spotify playlist run and a final review of working-tree changes before commit and push.
next_steps: Run MIX_SORTER against a real Spotify playlist, fix any runtime issues exposed by the live execution, and then review local_db.json plus the working-tree changes for a commit-ready push.

summary: Implemented a robust manual-paste Spotify OAuth flow for WSL2/Windows, verified the CLI with cargo fmt/test/clippy, completed a real run against playlist 5vNTVoJw5ZsdEgrDJgjCfd, and rewrote the README to match the actual product behavior and auth workflow.
next_steps: Commit and push the current changes; if you want better coverage on this playlist later, extend local_db.json for the 26 unmatched tracks.

summary: Integrated Exportify CSV support into the Rust CLI as an optional metadata overlay, added parser coverage and source-priority behavior, and verified the implementation with cargo fmt, cargo test, and cargo clippy. Evaluated the two repositories referenced in MIX_SORTER issue #1 and documented why spicetify-dj-info and audioFlux are not direct fits for the current architecture, while tightening the README around the accepted metadata strategy. No commits were made during the session.
next_steps: Review the intentional working-tree changes, sync the Orchestrator track plan and metadata with the already completed live playlist validation, then commit and push the Exportify and documentation work. If you want broader metadata coverage later, extend local_db.json using trusted Exportify imports and curated corrections.
